title: cyanea purpurellifolia
intro: Cyanea purpurellifolia, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant endemic to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its bright purple flowers and shrub-like growth. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of fever. The health benefits of Cyanea purpurellifolia include its antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional Hawaiian herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
